title: Implement jit serve command for background API and web UI server
description:
Add a `jit serve` command to the jit CLI that starts the jit-server API and web UI as a detached background process (not tied to the terminal), with PID-file-based collision detection and automatic port selection for multi-repo hosts.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `jit serve` starts the jit-server binary as a daemon process (detached from terminal)
- `jit serve --stop` stops the server for the current repo
- `jit serve --status` shows whether the server is running (port, PID, uptime)
- Multiple repos on the same host can run servers simultaneously (auto port selection 3000–3099)
- If server already running for this repo, `jit serve` prints its info and exits 0 (idempotent)
- Stale PID files (dead process) are cleaned up automatically
- `--json` flag provides machine-readable output
- `jit serve` is excluded from default MCP tool listing (hidden flag in schema)
- `crates/server` is enhanced with `--web-dir` to serve built web UI static files

## Command Interface
```
jit serve [--port PORT] [--stop] [--status] [--fg] [--log FILE] [--json]
```

## PID File
`.jit/server.pid.json` — stores pid, port, started_at, data_dir, log_file

## Output
- Start: `Server started on http://localhost:3000 (PID 12345)`
- Already running: `Server is already running on http://localhost:3000 (PID 12345)`
- Stop: `Server stopped (was PID 12345 on port 3000)`
- Status: `Server is running: http://localhost:3000 (PID 12345, started ...)`
